title: Support Docker’s non-OCI health check in OCI images?
body:
### Issue Description

This is maybe related to #3525 and #17817

Basically when starting this image, the built-in health check appears ignored: 

```
ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-server:v1.128.0
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman run ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-server:v1.128.0`
2. podman inspect ...


### Describe the results you received

no status of healthcheck

### Describe the results you expected

healthcheck reported

### podman info output

### Additional environment details

```

Wants=immich-machine-learning.service

[Container]

EnvironmentFile=/media/.../immich.env
Image=ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-server:v1.128.0
PublishPort=8222:2283
Network=immich
StopTimeout=900
GroupAdd=keep-groups
AddDevice=/dev/dri:/dev/dri
SecurityLabelLevel=s0:c555,c666
Volume=/media/...
Volume=/etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target

[Service]
TimeoutStartSec=900
TimeoutStopSec=950
Restart=always
```

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
The image is an OCI image and oci images do not have an healtcheck config so it is impossible to specify one there.

I don't know what immich does but if their image contains a healtcheck it must be in docker format otherwise it will be ignored as healtchecks are not part of an OCI image.

Mhh, looking at the oci spec it seems to list healtcheck as reserved in the config now, but only because docker uses it. It doesn't seem to be actually standardized, https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/issues/749

As such podman could in theory parse that value (and builds could set it instead of ignoring it).
`skopeo inspect --config --raw docker://ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-server:v1.128.0 | jq .config` shows it is actually defined while `skopeo inspect --config docker://ghcr.io/immich-app/immich-server:v1.128.0 | jq .config` shows that c/image is not parsing that (likely because it is not actually defined in the spec)

cc @mtrmac

Docker has, in the past few years, introduced https://github.com/moby/docker-image-spec/blob/main/specs-go/v1/image.go , an “extension” of OCI which includes health checks and other fields.

(Note that `DockerOCIImageMediaType` is the old v2s2 value, and it is not actually _referenced_ anywhere in the `moby/moby` codebase.)

This puts strict OCI consumers into an annoying position. Pragmatically, I guess there is demand for supporting that…

How strange to just close this issue. At my workplace, we have the following issue:

When building an image using `docker buildx build (...) --push`, the healthchecks are picked up by podman. But when we use `docker buildx build (...) --builder <dedicated_builder> --push`, they do NOT. The dedicated builder is created without any special flags, and the reason we sometimes NEED to use it, is to have support for multiplatform images. 

So given this, we're stuck with either a single-platform image built by the (outdated) built-in docker build builder , and have healthchecks working, OR we use multi-platform images built by the (up-to-date) builder, but lack healthchecks.

With the market for ARM64 dev+cloud machines having grown so large, there is a serious demand for multiplatform images; which apparently (we do nothing out of the ordinary) don't fully work with podman... I can imagine that being a reason to revert to docker at some point in time. Manually having to re-add health checks in our k8s/compose/service files is maintenance hell...
Reopening to let Podman maintainers _possibly_ reconsider. I don’t have a strong opinion on this.

There seems to be a connection:
- https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/18904
Yes, that looks like a duplicate.

I think that the ability to monitor containers is a must-have nowadays to run production workloads, and supporting the HEALTHCHECK stanza is a very convenient way to do it, and it also improves docker compatibility on this important field.

Would it be possible to have this in the TODO list please ?
